desc: Belize City is Belize's main transport gateway and a practical base for history stops, river excursions, and quick transfers to cayes or inland rainforest lodges.
hls: Most travelers use Belize City as an arrival/departure hub for flights, water taxis, and overland connections.
Best weather balance usually sits in the drier months from roughly February-April.
Good short-stay value for museum visits, Creole food, and day trips to river or Maya-heritage sites.
Easy onward links to Caye Caulker/San Pedro ferries and to inland hubs like San Ignacio.
City sightseeing works best with daylight planning and pre-arranged transport between neighborhoods.
Pairs well as a 1-2 night logistical stop before reef diving, island stays, or jungle lodges.

todo.name: Museum of Belize
todo.desc: Start with the central museum to cover colonial-era context, archaeology highlights, and national history.

todo.name: Swing Bridge + downtown walk
todo.desc: Do a short daytime loop around the historic core, St. John's Cathedral area, and waterfront landmarks.

todo.name: Belize River wildlife cruise
todo.desc: Join a half-day river excursion for mangrove scenery, birdlife, and possible manatee/crocodile sightings.

todo.name: Altun Ha day trip
todo.desc: Take a guided inland visit to the Maya site of Altun Ha with enough time for museum context on return.

todo.name: Caye Caulker transit day
todo.desc: Use the water taxi for a same-day taste of island pace if your wider itinerary allows.

todo.name: Creole food dinner
todo.desc: Reserve one evening for local dishes such as rice and beans, stew chicken, and fresh seafood.

wifi.note: Adequate in business hotels and some cafés; outages and weather-related interruptions can happen.

prac.fltNote: No UK nonstop; common routings connect via US or Central American hubs, and fares usually rise in UK school-holiday peaks plus Christmas/New Year.

airports.name: Philip S. W. Goldson International Airport

prac.currency: Belize Dollar (BZD), commonly pegged at 2 BZD = 1 USD; USD is widely accepted, but confirm change policy.
prac.lang: English is the official language; Belizean Creole and Spanish are also widely spoken.
prac.visa: Belize entry rules vary by passport and transit country; check visa requirements plus return/onward proof before departure.
alerts: Plan city walks in daylight and use known taxi operators for point-to-point travel.
Wet-season downpours can disrupt ferry comfort/timing and road transfers.
Book island-bound water taxi slots ahead during holiday peaks.

sweet: February to April is usually the most reliable weather window for combining urban logistics with day trips before shifting onward to the cayes or inland lodges.
